title: Find Relief with 10 Herbal Teas for Ankylosing Spondylitis
intro_desc: Herbal teas for ankylosing spondylitis are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of this chronic inflammatory disease by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving flexibility. The best herbal teas for ankylosing spondylitis include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Withania somnifera, Cinchona officinalis, Rheum palmatum, Echinacea purpurea, Achillea millefolium, and Cassia auriculata. If left untreated, ankylosing spondylitis can severely impact daily life, causing chronic pain, stiffness, and limited mobility, making simple tasks a challenge and affecting one's overall quality of life, social interactions, and career.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, contains a powerful compound called curcumin. This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which are very helpful for people with ankylosing spondylitis. Ankylosing spondylitis is a type of arthritis that causes pain and stiffness in the spine and other joints. The inflammation in these joints can be very uncomfortable and make it hard to move around. Curcumin in turmeric tea works by reducing the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. This helps to reduce the pain and stiffness in the joints, making it easier to move around and live with the condition. Additionally, curcumin has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to prevent further damage to the joints and slow down the progression of the disease. Some studies have also shown that curcumin can even help to reduce the risk of chronic pain and disability associated with ankylosing spondylitis.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for ankylosing spondylitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also promote bone health by preventing further fusion of vertebrae.

Polyphenols are good for ankylosing spondylitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also protect against cell damage, which can slow down the progression of the disease.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for ankylosing spondylitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce joint pain and swelling, which can help improve flexibility and reduce the risk of further damage in people with this condition.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin is good for ankylosing spondylitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also promote the healing of damaged tissues in the spine.

Tannins are good for ankylosing spondylitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can help slow down or even stop the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tea that helps with ankylosing spondylitis, start by gathering the right ingredients: dried Angelica sinensis root, which is also known as dong quai. Begin by rinsing a teaspoon of the dried root with clean water. Then, place the root in a teapot and pour in 8 ounces of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes, allowing the active ingredients to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the root. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink the tea plain to get the most benefits. It's recommended to drink 2 to 3 cups of Angelica sinensis tea per day to help manage ankylosing spondylitis symptoms, such as joint pain and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a popular remedy for people with ankylosing spondylitis. This condition causes inflammation and stiffness in the spine, making everyday activities painful. Ginger tea is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the joints and spine. The anti-inflammatory properties in ginger also help to reduce the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the progression of ankylosing spondylitis. Additionally, ginger has natural pain-relieving properties that can help to alleviate the chronic pain associated with this condition. By reducing inflammation and pain, ginger tea can help to improve the quality of life for people with ankylosing spondylitis, allowing them to move more comfortably and engage in activities they enjoy.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal teas that help with ankylosing spondylitis, start by gathering dried Angelica sinensis roots, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Begin by boiling 1-2 teaspoons of dried Angelica sinensis roots in 1 cup of water for about 10-15 minutes to allow the roots to release their medicinal properties. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for another 10-15 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste if desired. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis, a type of arthritis that causes inflammation and stiffness in the spine and joints. It's essential to note that Angelica sinensis is a blood thinner, so people taking blood thinners should consult their doctor before consuming it. Additionally, pregnant women and breastfeeding mothers should also consult their doctor before using Angelica sinensis herbal tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, may help people with ankylosing spondylitis (AS). This condition causes pain and stiffness in the spine and other joints. Glycyrrhiza glabra contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Inflammation is a big part of AS, so reducing it can help ease symptoms. Some studies suggest that glycyrrhizin can block the production of certain chemicals that cause inflammation, which may help to reduce pain and stiffness. Additionally, licorice root tea may also help to relax muscles and improve sleep quality, both of which are important for people with AS. However, it's essential to note that more research is needed to fully understand the benefits of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for AS. Some people with AS may also need to talk to their doctor before trying licorice root tea, as it can interact with certain medications.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tea that may help with ankylosing spondylitis, start by gathering the right ingredients: dried Angelica sinensis roots, a pot, and a tea infuser or strainer. Begin by boiling water in the pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one teaspoon of dried Angelica sinensis roots to the tea infuser or directly to the pot, depending on your preference. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid turns a deep yellow color. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or other natural sweeteners if desired to make the tea more palatable. For ankylosing spondylitis, it is recommended to drink 2-3 cups of this herbal tea per day, ideally in the morning and evening, to help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms. You can also store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and reheat it as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a natural remedy that may help people with ankylosing spondylitis. Ginkgo biloba also improves blood flow to the joints, which can help reduce fatigue and promote healing. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal tea that helps with ankylosing spondylitis, start by gathering dried Angelica sinensis roots or rhizomes. You can find these at a local health food store or online. Rinse the Angelica roots with filtered water to remove any dirt or impurities. Then, place one teaspoon of the Angelica roots in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour in 8 ounces of boiling water over the Angelica roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time, carefully lift the tea infuser or cup from the water and discard the Angelica roots. Strain the tea into another cup if necessary, and your Angelica sinensis herbal tea is ready to drink. You can enjoy this tea hot or iced, sweetened with honey if desired. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of Angelica sinensis herbal tea per day to help alleviate symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis. However, consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist before making any changes to your diet or treatment plan.

supplementary_best: A combination of herbal teas that may help with ankylosing spondylitis symptoms is a blend of 'Withania somnifera', 'Rheum palmatum', 'Harpagophytum procumbens', 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', and 'Salix alba'.  'Withania somnifera' is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. 'Rheum palmatum' contains rhubarb, a natural anti-inflammatory and laxative that can help soothe digestive issues often associated with ankylosing spondylitis. 'Harpagophytum procumbens', or devil's claw, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help with joint pain and swelling.  'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root, can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues. 'Salix alba', or white willow bark, contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that can help reduce pain and inflammation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have ankylosing spondylitis (AS), some herbal teas can make your condition worse. One such tea is feverfew, which contains parthenolide. This compound can slow down blood clotting and interact with blood-thinning medications, which people with AS often take to manage their condition. Another tea to avoid is licorice root, as it can increase blood pressure and worsen kidney problems, both of which are common in people with AS.  St. John's Wort is also not recommended for people with AS. It can interact with certain medications, such as nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) and corticosteroids, which are commonly used to treat AS symptoms. Furthermore, St. John's Wort can reduce the effectiveness of these medications.  Ephedra is another herbal tea that should be avoided. It contains ephedrine, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, potentially worsening AS symptoms. Additionally, ephedra can interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and medications for high blood pressure.  Finally, the tea goldenseal contains a compound called berberine, which can interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, and worsen kidney problems. People with AS should avoid these interactions by steering clear of goldenseal tea.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with ankylosing spondylitis, some natural remedies can help manage their symptoms. Epsom salt baths can be helpful as the magnesium in the salt can relax muscles and reduce inflammation. When you soak in an Epsom salt bath, the magnesium is absorbed through your skin, which can help reduce pain and stiffness in your joints. This can be especially useful for people who have trouble moving or exercising due to their condition.  Another natural remedy that can help is apple cider vinegar. Some people find that drinking apple cider vinegar helps reduce their symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Apple cider vinegar contains acetic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and improve digestion, which is often a problem for people with ankylosing spondylitis. Drinking apple cider vinegar can also help regulate your body's pH levels, which can be beneficial for people with ankylosing spondylitis who often have acidic bodies.  Turmeric is also a natural remedy that can help with ankylosing spondylitis. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin can help reduce pain and inflammation in the body, which can be beneficial for people with ankylosing spondylitis. Drinking turmeric tea or taking turmeric supplements can help reduce your symptoms and improve your quality of life.  Omega-3 fatty acids are also important for people with ankylosing spondylitis. Omega-3 fatty acids are anti-inflammatory and can help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. You can get omega-3 fatty acids from foods like salmon, sardines, and flaxseeds, or take supplements. Omega-3 fatty acids can help reduce your symptoms and improve your quality of life.  Ginger is also a natural remedy that can help with ankylosing spondylitis. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. You can drink ginger tea or eat ginger to help reduce your symptoms. Ginger can also help improve digestion and reduce nausea, which can be beneficial for people with ankylosing spondylitis.  These natural remedies can be used in combination with traditional treatments to help manage the symptoms of ankylosing spondylitis.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with ankylosing spondylitis (AS) often experience other health problems that can be helped with medicinal herbs. Due to inflammation in the spine, many develop iritis (inflammation of the iris) or uveitis (inflammation of the uvea), which can be treated with herbs like turmeric, containing curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. Turmeric also helps with skin conditions like psoriasis and eczema, which some AS patients develop due to the immune system's overactive response. Inflammation in the joints can lead to conditions like osteoporosis, which can be managed with herbs like ashwagandha, which helps maintain bone density and strengthens the body. Many AS patients also experience fatigue, which can be alleviated with herbs like ginseng, an adaptogen that enhances energy levels and reduces stress. Some AS patients develop heart problems like aortic aneurysms, which can be helped with herbs like ginger, a natural blood thinner. Additionally, many experience depression and anxiety, which can be treated with herbs like St. John's Wort, a natural mood stabilizer.
